data.body:
PASS-1 review (T1 prior-art/anti-repackaging overlay) — coaching note on this kill-switch/rollback remediation plan:

The plan's core scope (a "project-level disable-the-gate config; gate off => claim behaves exactly as today; fail-open" + a "documented rollback/back-out path") appears to already EXIST in the codebase, and the same T12 finding was already adjudicated and dismissed:

- `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` is an implemented, default-`false`, fail-open config flag (src/rebar/config.py:260; src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:651; rebar.toml:21) with tests asserting OFF => claim unchanged (tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_gate.py:233-239).
- docs/plan-review-gate.md:180 documents it verbatim as the rollback: "**Turning it off is the rollback** — an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed." §"Fail-open behavior" (lines 196-210) further documents default-off behavior and the claim-gate fail-closed-when-enabled stance.
- docs/experiments/plan-review-finalize-and-threepass.md:114-121 records the human adjudication of this exact finding: "the finding was an advisory **over-call** — the gate is **config-gated and fail-open when off**, so disabling it via config IS the kill-switch / rollback ... no separate rollback mechanism is needed by design"; the prematurely-filed follow-on `ce75` was "closed resolved-by-design."

Recommend the plan address this prior art before committing new scope: either (a) cite the existing flag + docs and re-scope to the genuine *residual* gaps the adjudication did NOT close — notably the persisted-artifact rollback for the NEW event types (REVIEW_RESULT sidecar + SIGNATURE) under a version downgrade, and deploy ordering/old+new coexistence, which final-gate notes (final_gate_cycle3.json:1182-1190, final_gate_3pass.json:97-107) flag as the real unaddressed risk — or (b) justify why the existing config-disable is insufficient as the system-level kill switch. Re-implementing the config flag as new work would be repackaging.
